g.data-narration: Welcome to morphological processing - the fundamental technique for analyzing and manipulating shapes in images.
Morphological processing analyzes image shapes by probing with a structuring element. It extracts shape information for image analysis.
g.text: A technique that probes images with a
structuring element to analyze shape and

g.data-narration: The structuring element is a small kernel that probes the image. Its shape and size critically determine the operation's outcome.
g.text: A small matrix/kernel that probes the image.
The origin (center) acts as anchor point.

g.data-narration: Erosion shrinks objects by keeping only pixels where the structuring element fits entirely inside the foreground.
g.text: A pixel is foreground ONLY if the SE fits
SE translated by z must be contained in A

g.data-narration: Watch erosion shrink the 3x3 square to just one pixel at the center. The outer layer gets removed because the SE doesn't fit.
Erosion removes small noise, separates connected objects, and isolates individual elements by shrinking them.
Dilation expands objects by adding pixels wherever the structuring element overlaps with any foreground pixel.
g.text: A pixel is foreground if the SE
OVERLAPS at least one foreground pixel.

g.data-narration: Watch dilation grow a single pixel into a 3x3 block. The SE adds a layer wherever it touches the original pixel.
Dilation fills holes, reconnects broken parts, and makes small features more prominent by expanding them.
For grayscale images, erosion finds local minimum (darkens), dilation finds local maximum (brightens). Same principle, different effect.
These dual properties are essential: erosion and dilation are mathematical opposites through complement operation.
Compare erosion and dilation: erosion shrinks by fitting entirely, dilation expands by overlapping - they are dual operations.
Remember these exam tips: Erosion fits entirely (shrinks), Dilation overlaps (expands). Use duality to solve problems faster.
In summary: Morphological operations use a structuring element to shrink (erosion) or expand (dilation) objects in images.
g.text: Morphological operations form the foundation of shape-based image analysis.
Erosion shrinks by fitting entirely; Dilation expands by overlapping.

g.data-narration: These fundamental operations enable opening, closing, and more complex morphological transforms used in real-world computer vision applications.
g.text: Erosion ⊖ and Dilation ⊕ combine to form Opening (Erosion→Dilation)
